title: RRSP Contribution Limit & Deduction Calculator 2026
description: Calculate your 2026 RRSP contribution limit and deduction room. Learn how the 18% rule and $32,490 max apply to your income with our interactive tool.

mainEntity.name: What is the RRSP contribution limit for 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The maximum RRSP contribution limit for the 2026 tax year is $32,490. Your personal limit is calculated as 18% of your earned income from 2025, up to this maximum amount, plus any unused contribution room carried forward from previous years.

mainEntity.name: How is my 2026 RRSP deduction limit calculated?

acceptedAnswer.text: Your 2026 RRSP deduction limit is calculated by taking 18% of your 2025 earned income (up to the maximum of $32,490) and subtracting any pension adjustments from your employer. You then add any unused contribution room from previous years.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I overcontribute to my RRSP?

acceptedAnswer.text: The CRA allows a lifetime overcontribution buffer of $2,000. If you exceed your RRSP contribution limit by more than $2,000, you will face a penalty tax of 1% per month on the excess amount until it is withdrawn or new room becomes available.

mainEntity.name: When is the deadline for 2026 RRSP contributions?

acceptedAnswer.text: The deadline for making RRSP contributions that can be deducted against your 2026 tax year income is generally March 1, 2027 (or February 28/29 if it's a leap year, adjusted for weekends).

mainEntity.name: How does the RRSP room calculator work?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our RRSP room calculator multiplies your prior year's earned income by 18%, compares it to the CRA maximum limit ($32,490 for 2026), and outputs your exact new contribution room. It also calculates your estimated tax savings based on your marginal tax bracket.
